metadata.description: Airline pet cargo and cabin policies for 22 airlines serving P1 routes (Block 3 initial + Block 7 expansion)
metadata.sources_note: Data sourced from airline websites where accessible. Many airline sites (Emirates, Singapore Airlines, Qantas, United, American, Air France, Cathay Pacific, Etihad, Air Canada) use JavaScript-heavy rendering that blocked automated extraction. For those airlines, data compiled from their published policy documentation and IATA Live Animals Regulations framework. Direct-scraped data marked with [SCRAPED]. All data should be verified against current airline policies before content publication.
direct_scraped_sources: CDC dog import requirements cross-referenced (accessed Apr 2026) [SCRAPED]

iata_framework.description: International Air Transport Association (IATA) Live Animals Regulations (LAR) set the baseline standards all airlines must comply with for animal transport.

key_standards.container_requirement_1: Pets must travel in IATA-compliant containers allowing the animal to stand, sit upright, lie down, and turn around without touching top or sides.
key_standards.ventilation: Containers must have adequate ventilation on at least 3 sides
key_standards.labelling: Live animal stickers, this-way-up arrows, shipper/consignee details required on container
key_standards.food_water: Instructions for 24-hour feeding/watering must accompany animal. Food and water offered within 4 hours of check-in.
key_standards.health_certificate: Veterinary health certificate required within 10 days of travel (varies by airline)
key_standards.sedation: IATA and virtually all airlines prohibit sedation/tranquilization of pets for air travel

emirates.name: Emirates

cabin_policy.exception: Falcons allowed in Economy Class cabin on flights to/from select destinations (UAE, Pakistan, some Middle East routes). Service/assistance animals not permitted in cabin.
cabin_policy.note: Emirates does NOT allow dogs or cats in the passenger cabin on any route.

cargo_policy.service: Emirates SkyCargo handles all pet transport as manifested cargo
cargo_policy.species: Dogs, cats, and birds (subject to route and country restrictions)
cargo_policy.crate_requirements: IATA-compliant rigid containers. Must allow animal to stand, turn, lie down.
cargo_policy.booking: Must be booked through Emirates SkyCargo or approved pet relocation agent
restrictions: Snub-nosed (brachycephalic) breeds accepted with additional requirements and seasonal restrictions
Temperature embargoes during extreme heat (summer months in Gulf region)
Some breed restrictions apply

emirates.fees: Varies by route, weight, and container size. Contact SkyCargo for quotes.
emirates.key_consideration: As a Dubai-hub airline, Emirates is the primary carrier for UAE pet relocations. All pets travel as cargo, never cabin.

british_airways.name: British Airways

cabin_policy.note: British Airways does NOT carry pets in the cabin on ANY route. Only assistance dogs travel in cabin (free of charge).

cargo_policy.service_from_uk: Partnership with PetAir UK (run by qualified veterinary surgeons, operating since 2004). Handles all pet exports from UK.
cargo_policy.service_to_uk: Partnership with IAG Cargo for pet imports to UK.
cargo_policy.species: Dogs, cats (and other animals through cargo partners)
cargo_policy.booking: Through PetAir UK (exports) or IAG Cargo (imports), not directly through BA passenger booking
cargo_policy.pet_travel_scheme: Must comply with UK DEFRA Pet Travel Scheme for UK entry

british_airways.emotional_support_animals: NOT accepted in cabin on any BA-operated flight. Must travel as cargo pet.
british_airways.fees: Through partner companies (PetAir UK / IAG Cargo). Varies by route, animal size, destination requirements.
british_airways.key_consideration: BA's model requires specialist third-party agent for all pet travel. No direct pet booking through airline. Good for complex UK regulatory requirements.

lufthansa.name: Lufthansa

cabin_policy.container: Soft-sided, waterproof, ventilated. Max dimensions 55 x 40 x 23 cm.
cabin_policy.fee_type: Extra cost as additional carry-on baggage
restrictions: Subject to country import/export regulations
Registration deadlines apply (must register pet before flight)
Not permitted into Lufthansa lounges
Subject to restrictions on flights with multiple airlines (codeshares)

cargo_policy.hold_as_excess_baggage: Larger dogs and cats can travel in air-conditioned cargo hold as excess baggage on passenger aircraft
cargo_policy.cargo_freight: Lufthansa Cargo offers transport of pets and other species as air freight
restrictions: Transport of large animals to certain destinations not possible due to local circumstances
Fighting dog breeds subject to special rules
Snub-nosed (brachycephalic) animals subject to additional requirements

lufthansa.us_dog_travel_note: Special CDC requirements apply when traveling to USA with dogs
lufthansa.fees: Varies by route and whether cabin or cargo. Contact Lufthansa for specific pricing.
lufthansa.key_consideration: One of the most pet-friendly major airlines. Allows cabin travel for small pets AND cargo hold on same aircraft. Lufthansa Cargo provides dedicated freight option.

singapore_airlines.name: Singapore Airlines
singapore_airlines.source: Singapore Airlines published policy (website extraction failed)

cabin_policy.note: Singapore Airlines does NOT allow pets in the passenger cabin (service dogs excepted on some routes).

cargo_policy.service: Pets travel as cargo in temperature-controlled hold
cargo_policy.booking: Must book through Singapore Airlines cargo or approved pet agent
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds subject to restrictions
Temperature embargoes apply on certain routes/seasons
Must comply with Singapore NParks AVS import regulations for pets entering Singapore

singapore_airlines.fees: Varies by route, animal weight, and container size
singapore_airlines.key_consideration: Singapore has strict AVS Category system (A/B/C/D). SIA cargo is the primary option for Singapore-bound pets.

qantas.name: Qantas

cabin_policy.note: Qantas does NOT allow pets in the passenger cabin on any route (assistance animals excepted on domestic flights).

cargo_policy.domestic: Qantas Freight handles domestic pet transport within Australia
cargo_policy.international: International pet transport through Qantas Freight as manifested cargo
cargo_policy.crate_requirements: IATA-compliant containers. Specific Qantas container specifications may apply.
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted year-round
Temperature embargoes: seasonal restrictions during Australian summer (Dec-Feb)
Australia's strict DAFF quarantine rules mean all international arrivals face minimum 10-day quarantine
Only arriving from DAFF-approved countries
cargo_policy.australia_specific: All pets entering Australia must have valid DAFF biosecurity import permit and meet all country-group-specific requirements. Qantas works with licensed pet transport agents.

qantas.fees: Varies by route. Contact Qantas Freight.
qantas.key_consideration: Australia's strict biosecurity regime means Qantas pet cargo is tightly regulated. Most customers use professional pet transport agents. No cabin option.

klm.name: KLM Royal Dutch Airlines

cabin_policy.container: Soft bag/carrier that fits under seat

cargo_policy.service: Pets can travel in the hold as checked baggage or via KLM Cargo
cargo_policy.documents: Travel documents required as per destination country regulations

klm.assistance_dogs: Trained service dogs allowed in cabin
klm.fees: Varies by route and cabin/hold option
klm.key_consideration: KLM part of Air France-KLM group. Amsterdam is major European transit hub.

air_france.name: Air France
air_france.source: Air France published policy (website extraction failed)

cabin_policy.weight_limit: Max 8 kg including soft bag (cabin). Dogs under 8 kg and cats.
cabin_policy.container: Soft, ventilated bag max 46 x 28 x 24 cm
cabin_policy.restricted_breeds: Dogs classified as Category 1 attack dogs not allowed

cargo_policy.hold: Dogs 8-75 kg travel in hold as checked baggage in IATA-compliant rigid kennel
cargo_policy.freight: Air France Cargo/KLM Cargo for larger shipments
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds subject to restrictions/seasonal embargoes
French regulations on dangerous dog breeds apply

air_france.fees: Cabin from approx EUR 40-125 depending on route. Hold varies by weight and route.
air_france.key_consideration: Part of Air France-KLM group. Good European hub coverage. France has breed-specific legislation.

united.name: United Airlines
united.source: United Airlines published policy (website extraction failed)

cabin_policy.species: Dogs, cats, rabbits, household birds (domestic US)
cabin_policy.weight_limit: Pet + carrier must fit under seat. No specific published weight limit but carrier max dimensions approx 17.5 x 12 x 7.5 inches (hard) or 18 x 11 x 11 inches (soft).
restrictions: Brachycephalic dogs/cats restricted from cargo but allowed in cabin if they fit
International: varies by route, some destinations cargo only

cargo_policy.note: United PetSafe cargo program was SUSPENDED indefinitely (since 2018/2019). Pets can only travel in-cabin on United.
cargo_policy.exception: Active US military with PCS orders may have options through United Cargo

united.key_consideration: CRITICAL: United does NOT offer cargo pet transport for regular customers. In-cabin only. This is a major limitation for large dogs or routes where cabin pets aren't permitted.

delta.name: Delta Air Lines
delta.hub: Atlanta (ATL), New York JFK, Los Angeles (LAX), Detroit (DTW), Minneapolis (MSP), Seattle (SEA)

requirements.kennel: Soft-sided, ventilated. Recommended max 18 x 11 x 11 inches. Must fit under seat.
requirements.pets_per_kennel: 1 pet per kennel (exceptions: unweaned litter with mother; 2 same-breed/size pets 8wk-6mo)
requirements.seating_restrictions: No bulkhead, emergency exit, no-stowage seats, flat-bed/Delta One seats, or specific rows on A330-200/300 and B757-200 center seats

cabin_policy.booking: First-come, first-serve. Call Delta Reservations. Limited spots per flight.

cargo_policy.allowed: Limited - currently only for active US Military or US State Dept FSO with PCS orders

normal_operations.booking: Through Delta Cargo Customer Service Center, separate from passenger booking
normal_operations.lead_time: Cannot book until 14 days prior to departure
normal_operations.arrival_at_facility: 4-6 hours prior domestic, 6 hours prior international
normal_operations.international_requirement: Must use IPATA-approved pet shipper for international cargo

health_requirements.health_certificate: From USDA-accredited vet within 10 days of travel. International: must be signed/endorsed by government vet authority.
health_requirements.sedation: Prohibited. No tranquilizers or sedating medication permitted.
health_requirements.feeding: Food and water offered within 4 hours of check-in. 24-hour feeding instructions required.

breed_restrictions.brachycephalic: Snub-nosed dogs, cats, and their mixes NOT permitted on Delta or Delta Connection flights as cargo

temperature_restrictions.max_ground_temp: 80F (27C) - no travel if exceeded at any point
temperature_restrictions.cold_acclimation: Certificate of Acclimation required for 20-45F (-7 to 7C)

fees.cabin_domestic: $95 (tickets before Apr 8, 2025) / $150 (tickets on/after Apr 8, 2025) each way

delta.key_consideration: Delta has the most detailed published pet policy of major US airlines. Significant limitation: cargo currently restricted to military. Many P1 destinations (UK, UAE, Australia, South Africa, Hong Kong) are cabin-exception routes requiring cargo. This creates a gap for Delta on key routes.

american_airlines.name: American Airlines
american_airlines.hub: Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW), Charlotte (CLT), Miami (MIA), Philadelphia (PHL), Chicago (ORD)
american_airlines.source: American Airlines published policy (website extraction failed)

cabin_policy.container: Soft-sided carrier that fits under seat. Max dimensions vary by aircraft.
cabin_policy.fee: $150 USD each way (domestic and select international)
restrictions: Not available to/from some international destinations
Breed restrictions may apply
Limit per flight enforced

cargo_policy.service: American Airlines Cargo handles pet transport
cargo_policy.note: AA cargo pet program availability has fluctuated. Check current status.

american_airlines.fees: Cabin: $150 each way. Cargo: varies by route and weight.
american_airlines.key_consideration: Major oneworld carrier. Miami hub important for Latin America connections.

cathay_pacific.name: Cathay Pacific
cathay_pacific.source: Cathay Pacific published policy (website extraction failed)

cabin_policy.note: Cathay Pacific does NOT allow pets in the passenger cabin (service dogs excepted).

cargo_policy.service: Pets travel as manifested cargo in temperature-controlled hold
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted during summer months
Hong Kong AFCD import regulations must be met
Temperature embargoes seasonal

cathay_pacific.fees: Varies by route, weight, container size
cathay_pacific.key_consideration: Primary carrier for Hong Kong pet relocations. No cabin option. Hong Kong has its own import permit system through AFCD.

etihad.name: Etihad Airways

cabin_policy.exception: Falcons permitted in Economy cabin on select routes (similar to Emirates policy)
cabin_policy.note: Dogs and cats NOT permitted in passenger cabin.

cargo_policy.service: Pets travel as cargo through Etihad Cargo
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds subject to seasonal restrictions
Temperature embargoes during Abu Dhabi summer months
UAE import regulations apply

etihad.fees: Contact Etihad Cargo. Varies by route and animal size.
etihad.key_consideration: Alternative UAE carrier to Emirates. Same cargo-only policy for pets. Abu Dhabi hub serves as alternative to Dubai for pet transport.

air_canada.name: Air Canada
air_canada.source: Air Canada published policy (website extraction failed)

cabin_policy.container: Soft-sided ventilated carrier that fits under seat (max 23 x 40 x 55 cm)
restrictions: Not available on all international routes
Limit per flight

cargo_policy.service: Air Canada Cargo handles pet transport in heated/pressurized hold
cargo_policy.temperature: Seasonal restrictions. No travel below -12C or above 29C at any point.
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted from cargo year-round

air_canada.fees: Cabin: CAD 71-118. Cargo: varies by route, weight, destination.
air_canada.key_consideration: Good option for North America-UK/Europe routes. Canada classified as low-risk for CDC dog import purposes.

south_african_airways.name: South African Airways

restrictions: Limited international routes
Subject to route restrictions

cargo_policy.service: Pets travel as checked excess baggage in hold or through SAA cargo
cargo_policy.note: South Africa classified as high-risk for CDC dog rabies. Additional US entry requirements apply.

south_african_airways.key_consideration: Primary carrier for South Africa pet relocations. SA is CDC high-risk country, complicating US-bound pet travel.

turkish_airlines.name: Turkish Airlines

cabin_policy.weight_limit: Max 8 kg including carrier for cabin
cabin_policy.container: Soft carrier, max 23 x 30 x 40 cm

cargo_policy.hold: Pets 8-32 kg travel in hold as checked baggage in IATA container
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted in cargo
Temperature restrictions apply

turkish_airlines.key_consideration: Istanbul is a major connecting hub. Turkish Airlines is one of the most pet-accommodating airlines globally (cabin + cargo). Useful for complex routing.

swiss.name: Swiss International Air Lines

cabin_policy.container: Soft-sided, ventilated, max 55 x 40 x 23 cm

cargo_policy.service: Larger pets travel in hold. Swiss WorldCargo for freight.

swiss.key_consideration: Part of Lufthansa Group, similar pet policies. Good alternative European hub.

virgin_atlantic.name: Virgin Atlantic

cabin_policy.note: Virgin Atlantic does NOT allow pets in cabin (assistance dogs excepted).

cargo_policy.service: Pets transported through Virgin Atlantic Cargo
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted
Temperature embargoes

virgin_atlantic.key_consideration: London-based carrier. No cabin option. Uses specialist pet transport agents.

korean_air.name: Korean Air

cabin_policy.container: Soft or hard carrier max 40 x 55 x 20 cm

cargo_policy.service: Larger pets in hold as checked baggage or Korean Air Cargo

korean_air.key_consideration: Good Asian hub option for connections.

emirates_skycargo_detail.name: Emirates SkyCargo (cargo division)
emirates_skycargo_detail.description: Dedicated cargo service for Emirates. Handles vast majority of UAE pet relocations.
emirates_skycargo_detail.facilities: State-of-the-art animal handling facilities at Dubai (DXB)
emirates_skycargo_detail.key_routes: Global network including all P1 countries
emirates_skycargo_detail.booking: Through Emirates SkyCargo agents or licensed pet relocation companies
emirates_skycargo_detail.note: Emirates SkyCargo is the go-to for Dubai/UAE pet relocations given Emirates' no-cabin policy.

japan_airlines.name: Japan Airlines (JAL)

cabin_policy.exception: JAL does not allow pets in the passenger cabin on international flights
cabin_policy.domestic_note: Pets accepted in cabin on some JAL domestic routes within Japan

cargo_policy.service: JAL Pet Policy: pets transported as checked baggage in temperature-controlled hold
cargo_policy.species: Dogs, cats, and certain small animals
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds: French Bulldogs BANNED from cargo (May-October). English Bulldogs banned year-round.
Summer restrictions: May-October temperature embargoes on some routes
Maximum 2 pets per container for small animals

fees.international: Based on container size and route. Typically JPY 30,000-60,000+
fees.note: Fees quoted at check-in or via cargo booking

japan_airlines.key_consideration: Important connecting airline for Australia-bound pets transiting through Tokyo. Group 3 status (Japan) means no 180-day wait for AU entry.

thai_airways.name: Thai Airways

cabin_policy.notes: Thai Airways does not permit pets in the passenger cabin on any route

cargo_policy.service: Pets as checked baggage or Thai Cargo for larger animals
cargo_policy.crate: IATA-compliant container. Maximum dimensions apply per aircraft type.
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted during hot season (March-May)
Some breed restrictions apply
Temperature embargoes possible during extreme heat periods

thai_airways.key_consideration: Bangkok is a common transit point for Australia-bound pets from Europe and Middle East. Transit regulations apply.

garuda_indonesia.name: Garuda Indonesia

cabin_policy.container: Soft-sided carrier, must fit under seat
cabin_policy.routes: Cabin pets allowed on domestic and some international routes (check at booking)

cargo_policy.service: Larger pets transported as checked baggage in pressurised, temperature-controlled hold
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds: check specific restrictions
Weight limits apply per aircraft type
Pets not accepted on certain short-haul routes

garuda_indonesia.key_consideration: Relevant for Indonesia-Singapore and Indonesia-Australia routes. Indonesia pet export requirements add complexity.

philippine_airlines.name: Philippine Airlines

cabin_policy.weight_limit: Max 7 kg including carrier on domestic, carrier restrictions on international
cabin_policy.notes: Cabin pet acceptance varies by route and aircraft type. Confirm at booking.

cargo_policy.service: Pets as checked baggage or PAL Cargo
restrictions: Brachycephalic breeds restricted from cargo
Temperature restrictions during extreme heat
Some route restrictions apply

philippine_airlines.key_consideration: Philippines is a common origin for pets entering Singapore and Hong Kong. High OFW (overseas Filipino worker) mobility creates pet transport demand.

brachycephalic_breed_policy.universally_restricted_from_cargo: Virtually all airlines restrict or ban snub-nosed breeds from cargo holds due to respiratory risk at altitude
brachycephalic_breed_policy.cabin_option: Some airlines allow brachycephalic breeds in cabin if pet fits carrier requirements
brachycephalic_breed_policy.key_content_angle: Brachycephalic breed transport is one of the most searched topics in pet relocation. Major content opportunity.

summer_risk_routes: All Gulf routes (Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha) Jun-Sep
temperature_embargoes.general_rule: Most airlines: no cargo transport below -7C / 20F or above 27-30C / 80-85F ground temperature
temperature_embargoes.planning_impact: Temperature embargoes can delay pet relocations by months. Critical planning factor for route pages.

cost_ranges.cargo_long_haul: $500-3000+ USD depending on animal size, route, and requirements
cost_ranges.full_service_relocation: $2,000-10,000+ USD (includes agent fees, vet work, documentation, transport, quarantine if applicable)
cost_ranges.note: Costs vary enormously by route, airline, animal size, and regulatory requirements. Australia relocations typically most expensive due to quarantine.

key_insights_for_content: Gulf carriers (Emirates, Etihad) are cargo-only for pets. Content must address this clearly for UAE routes.
UK carriers (BA, Virgin) are cargo-only. Pet transport requires third-party specialist agents.
US carriers have fragmented policies: Delta cargo military-only, United cargo suspended, American varies. This creates confusion for consumers.
Australia/NZ routes: ALL airlines require cargo, plus mandatory quarantine. Longest and most complex process.
Temperature embargoes are a hidden cost that adds weeks/months to timelines. Major content angle.
Brachycephalic breeds face severe restrictions across virtually all airlines' cargo operations. High-search-volume topic.
IATA Container Requirement #1 is the universal standard. Content should explain this clearly.
EU pet passport system simplifies intra-EU travel significantly compared to long-haul international routes.
Professional pet transport agents (IPATA members) are effectively mandatory for complex international routes.
CDC regulations (Aug 2024 update) significantly changed US entry requirements for dogs. High-value content topic.
